questions.question: Are there any anomalies or outliers in the time series of methane emissions for the Waste sector in any country?

analysis.design_rationale: A boxplot allows visual identification of outliers for each year across all countries specifically in the 'Waste' sector. Since the schema has full time-series columns as individual fields, we summarize all countries for each year as a distribution and mark countries that are outliers according to IQR rules. This provides a clean overview and surfaces anomalies (outlying emissions values) over time, consistent with the analytical question.
analysis.target_insight: Which countries, in which years, had anomalously high or low Waste sector methane emissions compared to the global range for that year.

chart_encodings.y: Methane emissions (MTCO2e, numeric value in each year column)
chart_encodings.color: Red for marked outlier points, blue for all others
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Boxplot for per-year distribution, jittered points for actual country values (with outliers highlighted), grid for clarity

transformations: Filter df to rows where Sector == 'Waste'
Melt yearly columns ('1990' to '2018') into a long format: columns=['Country', 'Year', 'Methane Emissions']
For each 'Year', compute IQR (Q1, Q3) and determine outlier threshold (values < Q1-1.5*IQR or > Q3+1.5*IQR); flag country-year pairs as outlier if value is outside
Prepare per-year boxplot data (all emissions values for that year across all countries)
data_preparation_steps: df_melt = waste.melt(id_vars=['Country'], value_vars=[str(y) for y in range(1990, 2019)], var_name='Year', value_name='Methane Emissions')
For each Year, compute Q1, Q3, IQR, lower, upper bounds; assign boolean 'is_outlier' to df_melt
Split df_melt for plotting: per-year lists of emissions and outlier points
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\2.csv")
waste = df[df['Sector'] == 'Waste'].copy()
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990,2019)]
df_melt = waste.melt(id_vars=['Country'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Methane Emissions')
df_melt['Year'] = df_melt['Year'].astype(int)
boxplot_data = [df_melt[df_melt['Year'] == y]['Methane Emissions'].dropna().values for y in range(1990,2019)]
outlier_rows = []
for idx, y in enumerate(range(1990,2019)):
    vals = np.array(boxplot_data[idx])
    if len(vals) > 0:
        q1 = np.percentile(vals, 25)
        q3 = np.percentile(vals, 75)
        iqr = q3 - q1
        lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
        upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
        mask = (df_melt['Year']==y) & ((df_melt['Methane Emissions'] < lower) | (df_melt['Methane Emissions'] > upper))
        outlier_rows.append(df_melt[mask])
outlier_df = pd.concat(outlier_rows) if outlier_rows else pd.DataFrame(columns=df_melt.columns)
plt.figure(figsize=(14,6))
bp = plt.boxplot(boxplot_data, positions=np.arange(1990,2019), notch=True, patch_artist=True, showbox=True, whiskerprops={'color':'#999999'}, boxprops={'facecolor':'#aec6cf','color':'#333344'}, medianprops={'color':'#cc0000','linewidth':2}, showmeans=False, widths=0.6, flierprops={'marker': '', 'markersize':0})
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle=':', alpha=0.4)
plt.scatter(df_melt['Year'], df_melt['Methane Emissions'], color="#6b7caf", s=12, alpha=0.45, edgecolor='none')
if not outlier_df.empty:
    plt.scatter(outlier_df['Year'], outlier_df['Methane Emissions'], color='red', s=35, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='k', linewidths=0.45, label='Outliers')
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Methane Emissions (MTCO2e)")
plt.title("Yearly Boxplot of Methane Emissions in Waste Sector by Country\nOutliers Highlighted as Red Dots")
if not outlier_df.empty:
    plt.legend(loc='upper right')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

analysis.chart_type: Heatmap of per-country outlier-years (binary outlier matrix)
analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap provides a country-by-year grid where color intensity indicates whether a country is classified as an outlier for a given year in the Waste sector. This answers the anomaly identification question from a broad, matrix-style overview, making it easy to spot countries with persistent or episodic anomalies. Sorting countries by total number of outlier years further highlights those with repeated emissions anomalies.
analysis.target_insight: Which countries exhibit repeated or singular anomalies over time in the Waste sector; which years see most anomalies globally.

chart_encodings.y: Country (sorted by number of outlier years descending)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Heatmap (binary, no colorbar), grid for legibility, annotation of top 5 most anomalous countries

transformations: Melt yearly columns ('1990'-'2018') to long format: ['Country', 'Year', 'Methane Emissions']
For each Year, compute IQR boundaries and set flag is_outlier
Create a [Country x Year] matrix with 1 for outlier, 0 for normal
Sum is_outlier per country (across years) and sort Countries descending by total outlier count
data_preparation_steps: df_melt = waste.melt(id_vars=['Country'], value_vars=[str(y) for y in range(1990, 2019)], var_name='Year', value_name='Methane Emissions')
For each Year, compute IQR thresholds and assign 'is_outlier' column (1=outlier, 0=normal)
Count anomalies per country and sort index accordingly
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\2.csv")
waste = df[df['Sector'] == 'Waste'].copy()
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990,2019)]
df_melt = waste.melt(id_vars=['Country'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Methane Emissions')
df_melt['Year'] = df_melt['Year'].astype(int)
df_melt['is_outlier'] = 0
for y in range(1990,2019):
    vals = df_melt[df_melt['Year']==y]['Methane Emissions'].dropna().values
    if len(vals)>0:
        q1 = np.percentile(vals, 25)
        q3 = np.percentile(vals, 75)
        iqr = q3 - q1
        lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
        upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
        mask = (df_melt['Year']==y) & ((df_melt['Methane Emissions'] < lower) | (df_melt['Methane Emissions'] > upper))
        df_melt.loc[mask, 'is_outlier'] = 1
hm = df_melt.pivot(index='Country', columns='Year', values='is_outlier').fillna(0)
hm['OutlierSum'] = hm.sum(axis=1)
hm = hm.sort_values('OutlierSum', ascending=False)
hm = hm.drop('OutlierSum', axis=1)
plt.figure(figsize=(19,9))
cmap = mpl.colors.ListedColormap(['#ffffff','#c13c3c'])
plt.imshow(hm.values, aspect='auto', cmap=cmap, vmin=0, vmax=1)
plt.yticks(ticks=np.arange(hm.shape[0]), labels=hm.index, fontsize=8)
plt.xticks(ticks=np.arange(len(hm.columns)), labels=hm.columns, rotation=90)
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Country (sorted by anomaly count)")
title = "Heatmap of Outlier Years for Waste Methane Emissions\nRed=Year is Anomaly per IQR, White=Normal\nTop: Countries with Most Anomaly Years on Top"
plt.title(title)
plt.grid(axis='x', linestyle=':', color='#cccccc', alpha=0.15)
n_top = 5
for i, country in enumerate(hm.index[:n_top]):
    plt.text(0, i+0.2, '*', color='blue', fontsize=13, ha='left', va='center')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

table_metadata.description: Data loaded from CSV file: 2.csv (Dataset: Methane Emissions Around The World (1990-2018))

fields.name: Country
fields.sample_values: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Angola, ..., Vietnam, World, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the country or global region (e.g., "World") for which methane emission data is recorded, corresponding to national boundaries in the environmental domain.

fields.name: Sector
fields.sample_values: Agriculture, Energy, Fugitive Emissions, Industrial Processes, Land-Use Change and Forestry, Other Fuel Combustion, Total excluding LUCF, Total including LUCF, Waste
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field categorizes the source or activity sectors contributing to methane emissions, such as agriculture, energy, industry, and waste, within global environmental and climate reporting contexts.

fields.name: Gas
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the type of greenhouse gas measured, specifically methane (CH4), in global emissions data.

fields.name: Unit
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field specifies that methane emissions data are measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (MTCO2e), indicating standardized greenhouse gas emissions quantification for environmental and climate reporting.
